title: mangifera altissima
intro: Mangifera altissima, also known as the 'Wild Mango' or 'Benguet mango', is a plant species in the Anacardiaceae family, closely related to the mango tree but with a distinct set of characteristics. The leaves and bark of the Mangifera altissima plant have been used in traditional medicine for their purported medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of the plant include its potential to reduce fever, treat wounds, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark of Mangifera altissima can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
